title: Tier 2 — Concurrence, Réactivité et Optimisation
prompt:
You are taking a TIER 2 EXPERT evaluation — concurrency, reactive programming and optimization.

Solve 5 JavaScript exercises. Return your answers in Markdown using headers for the exercise IDs and code blocks for the JavaScript functions.

[2-A] Async function executerEnPool(taches, concurrence) — executes an array of async task functions with a concurrency limit. Returns an array of results in original order.
Example: executerEnPool([()=>fetchA(), ()=>fetchB(), ()=>fetchC()], 2) runs max 2 at a time.

[2-B] Function creerSubject() — a reactive Subject (like RxJS): has next(val), error(err), complete(), and subscribe({next, error, complete}). Multicasts to all subscribers.

[2-C] Function memoiserAsync(fn) — memoize an async function. Concurrent calls with the same args while the first is pending should wait for the same Promise, not trigger duplicate calls.

[2-D] Debugging — fix the following async race condition:
async function chargerUtilisateur(id) {
  let resultat = null;
  fetch('/api/user/' + id).then(r => r.json()).then(data => { resultat = data; });
  return resultat;
}
Bug: returns null because it doesn't await the fetch.

[2-E] Function creerCircuitBreaker(fn, seuil, delaiResetMs) — circuit breaker pattern: after seuil consecutive failures, trips open and rejects all calls for delaiResetMs ms, then moves to half-open.

Expected strict response format:
{
  "tache_2a": "async function executerEnPool(taches, concurrence) { ... }",
  "tache_2b": "function creerSubject() { ... }",
  "tache_2c": "function memoiserAsync(fn) { ... }",
  "tache_2d": "async function chargerUtilisateur(id) { ... }",
  "tache_2e": "function creerCircuitBreaker(fn, seuil, delaiResetMs) { ... }"
}

[ALGORITHMIC EXERCISES — code pur, sans culture générale]
Return each function using a Markdown header (### <exercise id>) followed by a ```javascript code block.

[EXERCISE algo_facile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "estPalindrome(s)" that returns true if the string reads the same backward (case-sensitive).

[EXERCISE algo_facile_2]
Write a JavaScript function "pgcd(a, b)" that returns the greatest common divisor of a and b (both > 0).

[EXERCISE algo_moyen_1]
Write a JavaScript function "nombreMots(s)" that returns the number of words separated by whitespace. Ignore leading/trailing spaces.

[EXERCISE algo_difficile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "fusionTriee(a, b)" that merges two already-sorted arrays into one sorted array.

[EXERCISE algo_defi]
Write a JavaScript function "sontAnagrammes(a, b)" that returns true if a and b are anagrams (same letters rearranged, case-sensitive).

evaluations.description: gestion des échecs partiels en pool

evaluations.call: (()=>{ var s=creerSubject(); var a=[],b=[]; s.subscribe({next:function(v){a.push(v);},error:function(){},complete:function(){}}); s.subscribe({next:function(v){b.push(v);},error:function(){},complete:function(){}}); s.next(1); s.next(2); return a.length===2 && b.length===2 && a[0]===1; })()

(()=>{ var s=creerSubject(); var done=0; s.subscribe({next:function(){},error:function(){},complete:function(){done++;}}); s.subscribe({next:function(){},error:function(){},complete:function(){done++;}}); s.complete(); return done; })()
(()=>{ var fn=memoiserAsync(async function(x){ return x*2; }); return fn(5).then ? 'ok' : 'fail'; })()

evaluations.description: appels concurrents partagent la même Promise
evaluations.call: (()=>{ var calls=0; var fn=memoiserAsync(async function(x){ calls++; return x; }); fn(1); fn(1); return calls; })()
(()=>{ var cb=creerCircuitBreaker(async function(){ return 'ok'; }, 3, 1000); return cb().then ? 'promise' : 'fail'; })()

(()=>{ var calls=0; var cb=creerCircuitBreaker(async function(){ throw new Error('fail'); }, 2, 10000); cb().catch(function(){}); cb().catch(function(){}); return cb().then(function(){ return 'ok'; }).catch(function(e){ return e.message; }); })()

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Palindrome
tasks.hint: Comparez s à son inverse : s.split("").reverse().join("").

tasks.label: Algorithmique — PGCD
tasks.hint: Algorithme d'Euclide : tant que b !== 0, [a, b] = [b, a % b] ; renvoyez a.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Fusion triée
tasks.hint: Deux pointeurs : comparez les têtes et prenez la plus petite à chaque pas.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Anagrammes
tasks.hint: Triez les caractères des deux chaînes et comparez-les.
